webhooks.name: clusterservingruntime.kserve-webhook-server.validator
webhooks.purpose: Validates ServingRuntime and ClusterServingRuntime resources on create/update to ensure no two runtimes sharing the same protocol version have duplicate model format priorities, that priorities for the same model format within a single runtime are consistent, and that multi-node WorkerSpec configurations are valid (e.g., GPU types, parallel sizes, container count, and disallowing removal of an existing WorkerSpec).

webhooks.name: inferenceservice.kserve-webhook-server.pod-mutator
webhooks.purpose: Mutates pods owned by KServe InferenceServices at creation time, injecting storage initializer init-containers (for model downloading with appropriate credentials), inference logger/batcher agent sidecars, metrics aggregator sidecars, GKE accelerator node selectors, Istio CNI security contexts, and optionally modelcar image volumes, so that model-serving pods are fully configured before they start.

webhooks.name: llminferenceservice.kserve-webhook-server.v1alpha1.validator
webhooks.purpose: Validating webhook for LLMInferenceService v1alpha1 that enforces structural and cross-field constraints on create and update. It rejects invalid router/gateway/HTTPRoute combinations, ensures parallelism settings are consistent (e.g., data and dataLocal must be paired, pipeline and data parallelism are mutually exclusive, worker requires parallelism), validates scheduler config (replicas > 0, exactly one of inline or ref), delegates scaling validation to the v1alpha2 hub type, and prevents mutation of the total parallelism size on updates.

webhooks.name: llminferenceservice.kserve-webhook-server.v1alpha2.validator
webhooks.purpose: Validating webhook for the LLMInferenceService v1alpha2 custom resource that enforces structural and cross-field constraints on create and update: router/gateway/HTTPRoute consistency, parallelism configuration rules (pipeline vs data parallelism mutual exclusivity, data/dataLocal co-requirement, positive values), immutability of total parallelism size across updates, scheduler config completeness (inline vs ref exclusivity), and autoscaling rules (scaling vs replicas exclusivity, min/max bounds, WVA actuator backend exclusivity between HPA and KEDA, actuator consistency across decode and prefill workloads, variantCost format, KEDA controller-owned field restrictions, and idleReplicaCount constraints).

webhooks.name: llminferenceserviceconfig.kserve-webhook-server.v1alpha1.validator
webhooks.purpose: Validates LLMInferenceServiceConfig resources on create and update, rejecting configs that use the forbidden baseRefs field or specify non-positive scheduler replicas, and emitting warnings when well-known (built-in) configs are modified or deleted.

webhooks.name: llminferenceserviceconfig.kserve-webhook-server.v1alpha2.validator
webhooks.purpose: Validates LLMInferenceServiceConfig resources on create and update, rejecting configs that use the forbidden baseRefs field or specify non-positive scheduler replicas, and emitting warnings when well-known (built-in) configs are modified or deleted.

webhooks.name: servingruntime.kserve-webhook-server.validator
webhooks.purpose: Validates ServingRuntime and ClusterServingRuntime resources on create/update to ensure no two runtimes sharing the same protocol version have duplicate model format priorities, that priorities for the same model format within a single runtime are consistent, and that multi-node WorkerSpec configurations are valid (e.g., GPU types, parallel sizes, container count, and disallowing removal of an existing WorkerSpec).

webhooks.name: inferenceservice.kserve-webhook-server.pod-mutator
webhooks.purpose: Mutates pods managed by KServe InferenceServices during creation by injecting storage initializer containers (for model downloading), credential volumes/env vars for accessing model storage, inference logger/batcher agent sidecars, metrics aggregator sidecars, GKE accelerator node selectors, and Istio CNI security contexts.

webhooks.name: llminferenceservice.kserve-webhook-server.v1alpha1.validator
webhooks.purpose: Validates LLMInferenceService resources on create and update, enforcing cross-field constraints on router/gateway/HTTPRoute configuration, parallelism rules (data, dataLocal, and pipeline parallelism cannot conflict and require a worker), scheduler config consistency (inline vs ref, replicas > 0), scaling/actuator backend consistency across decode and prefill workloads, and immutability of total parallelism size on updates.

webhooks.name: llminferenceservice.kserve-webhook-server.v1alpha2.validator
webhooks.purpose: Validates LLMInferenceService resources on create and update, enforcing cross-field constraints on router/gateway/HTTPRoute configuration, parallelism settings (pipeline vs data parallelism, worker requirements, immutability of parallelism size), scheduler config (inline vs ref, replicas), and autoscaling rules (HPA/KEDA mutual exclusivity, actuator consistency between decode and prefill workloads, WVA variant cost format, replica bounds).

webhooks.name: llminferenceserviceconfig.kserve-webhook-server.v1alpha1.validator
webhooks.purpose: Validates LLMInferenceServiceConfig resources on create and update, rejecting configs that use the forbidden baseRefs field or specify invalid scheduler replicas (must be >0), and emits warnings when users modify or delete well-known (built-in) configs.

webhooks.name: llminferenceserviceconfig.kserve-webhook-server.v1alpha2.validator
webhooks.purpose: Validates LLMInferenceServiceConfig resources on create and update by forbidding the baseRefs field (recursive refs are disabled), ensuring scheduler replicas are greater than zero, running any injected config validation function, and emitting warnings when well-known configs are modified or deleted.

webhooks.name: servingruntime.kserve-webhook-server.validator
webhooks.purpose: Validates ServingRuntime create/update requests by ensuring model format priorities are consistent within each runtime and unique across runtimes sharing the same protocol version, and by enforcing multi-node WorkerSpec constraints (parallel size bounds, single-container limit, GPU type validity, blocked env vars).

webhooks.name: notebooks-validation.opendatahub.io
webhooks.purpose: Validates updates to Notebook resources, specifically preventing removal of the MLflow instance annotation while a notebook is running. This guards against a broken state where MLflow environment variables persist in the pod but the associated RoleBinding has already been deleted, leaving the MLflow client with credentials but no permissions.

webhooks.name: notebooks.opendatahub.io
webhooks.purpose: Mutating admission webhook for Kubeflow Notebook CRs on create and update that injects operational sidecars and configuration: it resolves container images from OpenShift ImageStreams, injects a kube-rbac-proxy authentication sidecar, mounts trusted CA certificate bundles, mounts pipeline runtime image ConfigMaps, syncs and mounts Elyra/DSPA pipeline configuration secrets, injects MLflow tracking environment variables, injects cluster-wide proxy environment variables, adds a reconciliation lock annotation on creation, and blocks pod-template-changing mutations on running notebooks to prevent unintended restarts.

webhooks.name: mraycluster.kb.io
webhooks.purpose: Mutates RayCluster resources on create/update to enforce platform-specific networking defaults. On OpenShift clusters (detected via Route API availability), it enables the secure trusted network annotation and strictly disables enableIngress to enforce Gateway API-only access with centralized authentication; on non-OpenShift clusters, it sets the secure trusted network annotation to false.

webhooks.name: mraycluster.kb.io
webhooks.purpose: Mutates RayCluster resources on create/update to enforce platform-specific networking defaults. On OpenShift clusters (detected via Route API availability), it enables the secure trusted network annotation and strictly disables enableIngress to enforce Gateway API-only access with centralized authentication; on non-OpenShift clusters, it sets the secure trusted network annotation to false.

webhooks.name: vraycluster.kb.io
webhooks.purpose: Validates RayCluster resources on create and update, ensuring the cluster name matches a DNS-compatible regex pattern (lowercase alphanumeric, starting with a letter) and that all worker group names within the spec are unique.

webhooks.name: mmodelregistry.opendatahub.io
webhooks.purpose: Mutating and validating admission webhooks for ModelRegistry custom resources. The mutating webhook applies default values to ModelRegistry specs on create/update, while the validating webhook enforces spec correctness and ensures ModelRegistry names are unique across the entire cluster by listing all existing ModelRegistry resources during creation.

webhooks.name: vmodelregistry.opendatahub.io
webhooks.purpose: Mutating and validating admission webhooks for ModelRegistry custom resources. The mutating webhook applies default values to ModelRegistry specs on create/update, while the validating webhook enforces spec correctness and ensures ModelRegistry names are unique across the entire cluster by listing all existing ModelRegistry resources during creation.

webhooks.name: minferencegraph-v1alpha1.odh-model-controller.opendatahub.io
webhooks.purpose: Mutating webhook (defaulter) is a no-op placeholder, while the validating webhook ensures that every serviceURL referenced in an InferenceGraph's node steps uses a cluster-internal hostname within the same namespace as the InferenceGraph, rejecting cross-namespace references and public/external hostnames to prevent credential leakage.

webhooks.name: minferenceservice-v1beta1.odh-model-controller.opendatahub.io
webhooks.purpose: Mutating webhook is a no-op defaulter placeholder. Validating webhook rejects InferenceService creation when the target namespace matches the platform's applications namespace (read from DSCInitialization) to protect that namespace, and also validates that the InferenceService name does not exceed the allowed length.

webhooks.name: mutating.pod.odh-model-controller.opendatahub.io
webhooks.purpose: Mutates incoming Pods to inject Ray TLS infrastructure (volumes, volume mounts, and a certificate-copying init container) when the pod's kserve-container or worker-container has the RAY_USE_TLS environment variable set to a non-zero value, enabling mTLS communication between Ray cluster nodes used for model serving.

webhooks.name: validating.isvc.odh-model-controller.opendatahub.io
webhooks.purpose: Mutating webhook is a no-op defaulter placeholder. Validating webhook rejects InferenceService creation when the target namespace matches the platform's applications namespace (read from DSCInitialization) to protect that namespace, and also validates that the InferenceService name does not exceed the allowed length.

webhooks.name: validating.nim.account.odh-model-controller.opendatahub.io
webhooks.purpose: Validates NIM Account creation by enforcing a singleton constraint: only one Account resource is allowed per namespace. Update and delete operations are always permitted without validation.

webhooks.name: vinferencegraph-v1alpha1.odh-model-controller.opendatahub.io
webhooks.purpose: Mutating webhook (defaulter) is a no-op placeholder, while the validating webhook ensures that every serviceURL referenced in an InferenceGraph's node steps uses a cluster-internal hostname within the same namespace as the InferenceGraph, rejecting cross-namespace references and public/external hostnames to prevent credential leakage.

webhooks.name: mutate-pod.sparkoperator.k8s.io
webhooks.purpose: Mutating admission webhook that intercepts Pod create/update requests in Spark job namespaces, looks up the owning SparkApplication by label, and defaults the pod spec with Spark-specific configuration including volumes, volume mounts, environment variables, config maps (Spark, Hadoop, general, Prometheus), container ports, affinity, tolerations, node selectors, DNS config, scheduler name, priority class, security contexts, sidecar/init containers, GPU resources, host network settings, termination grace period, lifecycle hooks, host aliases, share process namespace, memory limits, and an owner reference to the SparkApplication.

webhooks.name: mutate-sparkapplication.sparkoperator.k8s.io
webhooks.purpose: Mutating webhook that sets default values on SparkApplication resources during create and update operations. It only applies defaults (via the operator's webhook scheme) when the application is in a 'New' or 'Invalidating' state, ensuring defaults are re-applied when a SparkApplication is first created or when its spec is being revalidated.

webhooks.name: validate-scheduledsparkapplication.sparkoperator.k8s.io
webhooks.purpose: Validates ScheduledSparkApplication resources on create and update, ensuring the metadata.name is a valid DNS-1035 label so that downstream Kubernetes Services and child SparkApplication resources can be created without naming failures.

webhooks.name: validate-sparkapplication.sparkoperator.k8s.io
webhooks.purpose: A mutating admission webhook defaulter for ScheduledSparkApplication resources that is intended to set default values on create/update, but currently only logs that mutation was invoked and performs no actual defaulting logic.

webhooks.name: connection-notebook.opendatahub.io
webhooks.purpose: Mutating webhook for Kubeflow Notebooks that processes the 'opendatahub.io/connections' annotation: it validates that referenced Secrets exist in the Notebook's namespace, verifies the requesting user has 'get' permission on each Secret via SubjectAccessReview, and then injects (or removes) those Secrets as envFrom secretRef entries in the Notebook's container spec.

webhooks.name: podmonitor-injector.opendatahub.io
webhooks.purpose: Mutates PodMonitor and ServiceMonitor resources on create/update by injecting the opendatahub.io/monitoring=true label when the resource's namespace is labeled for ODH monitoring and the cluster-scoped Monitoring CR exists, enabling Prometheus to discover these monitors for ODH-managed components.

webhooks.name: hardwareprofile-notebook-injector.opendatahub.io
webhooks.enable_condition: Kserve component enabled OR Workbenches component enabled
webhooks.purpose: Mutating admission webhook that intercepts create/update operations on Notebooks, InferenceServices, and LLMInferenceServices to inject hardware profile settings (resource requests/limits, node selectors, tolerations) from a referenced HardwareProfile CR into the workload's pod spec. It also handles profile switches (clearing old settings before applying new ones) and profile removal (cleaning up previously applied settings).

webhooks.name: connection-isvc.opendatahub.io
webhooks.purpose: Mutating webhook for KServe InferenceService resources that automatically injects data connection configuration (S3 storage keys, OCI image pull secrets, or URI storage URIs) into the ISVC spec based on a platform connection annotation referencing a Secret, and cleans up or replaces those injected fields when the annotation changes or is removed.

webhooks.name: connection-llmisvc.opendatahub.io
webhooks.purpose: Mutating webhook for LLMInferenceService resources that automatically injects model storage configuration (spec.model.uri, imagePullSecrets, serviceAccountName) based on a platform-connection annotation referencing a Secret. On create it injects the connection; on update it detects annotation changes and performs inject, remove, or replace of the storage-related fields for URI, OCI, and S3 connection types.

webhooks.name: dscinitialization-v2-validator.opendatahub.io
webhooks.purpose: Validates DSCInitialization v2 resources by enforcing singleton semantics on create (denies if an instance already exists) and preventing deletion while any DataScienceCluster object still exists, ensuring the initialization resource outlives all clusters that depend on it.

webhooks.name: datasciencecluster-v2-defaulter.opendatahub.io
webhooks.purpose: Mutating webhook that sets default field values on DataScienceCluster v2 resources during create and update. Specifically, when the ModelRegistry component is managed and its RegistriesNamespace is empty, it populates it with the default model registries namespace.

webhooks.name: datasciencecluster-v2-validator.opendatahub.io
webhooks.purpose: Validates DataScienceCluster v2 admission requests by enforcing a singleton constraint on creation (denying if another instance already exists) and rejecting any create or update that sets the Kueue component's managementState to Managed, which is no longer supported.

webhooks.name: dscinitialization-v1-validator.opendatahub.io
webhooks.purpose: Validates DSCInitialization v1 resources by enforcing singleton semantics on creation (denies if an instance already exists) and preventing deletion while any DataScienceCluster resource still exists, ensuring proper lifecycle ordering between the two CRDs.

webhooks.name: datasciencecluster-v1-defaulter.opendatahub.io
webhooks.purpose: Mutating webhook that sets default field values on DataScienceCluster v1 resources during create and update operations. Specifically, when the ModelRegistry component is enabled (Managed), it defaults the RegistriesNamespace field to the standard namespace if left empty.

webhooks.name: datasciencecluster-v1-validator.opendatahub.io
webhooks.purpose: Validates DataScienceCluster v1 admission requests by enforcing singleton creation (only one instance allowed), denying Kueue with ManagementState=Managed, and blocking v1 API updates when v2-only components are currently Managed to prevent silent data loss during v1-to-v2 conversion.

webhooks.name: vworkspace.kb.io
webhooks.purpose: Validates Workspace resources on create and update by fetching the referenced WorkspaceKind and ensuring the selected imageConfig and podConfig IDs exist within that WorkspaceKind's allowed values, and that pod template metadata (labels and annotations) conforms to Kubernetes validation rules.

webhooks.name: vworkspacekind.kb.io
webhooks.purpose: Validates WorkspaceKind resources on create, update, and delete. On create/update, it validates pod template metadata (labels/annotations), environment variables, default imageConfig/podConfig references, imageConfig values (port references and protocol-specific fields), and redirect chains (detecting missing targets and cycles). On update, it optimizes by only re-validating changed configs and checks usage counts to prevent removing or breaking options still in use by Workspaces. On delete, it blocks deletion if any Workspaces reference the WorkspaceKind or if the protection finalizer is present.

webhooks.name: localmodelnamespacecache.kserve-webhook-server.validator
webhooks.purpose: Validates LocalModelNamespaceCache resources on create, update, and delete. On create/update, it ensures all referenced node groups exist as LocalModelNodeGroup resources. On delete, it prevents removal if any InferenceService in the namespace still references the cache via label matching.

webhooks.name: validator.pytorchjob.training-operator.kubeflow.org
webhooks.purpose: Validates PyTorchJob resources on create and update, ensuring the job name is DNS-1035 compliant, replica specs contain valid replica types (Master/Worker) with correct replica counts (Master must be exactly 1), each replica has at least one container named 'pytorch' with an image defined, elastic policy is properly configured with a Worker spec, and RunPolicy fields are valid.

webhooks.name: validator.xgboostjob.training-operator.kubeflow.org
webhooks.purpose: Validates XGBoostJob resources on create and update, ensuring the job name is a valid DNS-1035 label, that replica specs contain only valid replica types (Master and Worker), that exactly one Master replica exists, that every container has an image defined, and that a default container named 'xgboost' is present in each replica spec.

webhooks.name: validator.tfjob.training-operator.kubeflow.org
webhooks.purpose: Validates TFJob resources on create and update, ensuring the job name is a valid DNS-1035 label, each replica spec contains at least one container with a non-empty image and a container named 'tensorflow', at most one Chief or Master role is defined, and RunPolicy fields are valid and not improperly mutated.

webhooks.name: validator.jaxjob.training-operator.kubeflow.org
webhooks.purpose: Validates JAXJob resources on create and update to ensure the job name is a valid DNS-1035 label, the spec contains only the supported 'Worker' replica type, each replica spec has at least one container with a defined image, and a container named 'jax' (the default) is present.

webhooks.name: validator.paddlejob.training-operator.kubeflow.org
webhooks.purpose: Validates PaddleJob resources on create and update, ensuring the job name is a valid DNS-1035 label, replica types are limited to Master and Worker, each replica spec has containers with images defined, and at least one container is named 'paddle'. On updates it also validates that the RunPolicy has not been modified in disallowed ways.

webhooks.name: validator.clustertrainingruntime.trainer.kubeflow.org
webhooks.purpose: Validates ClusterTrainingRuntime resources on create and update by checking that the replicated jobs in the runtime spec are well-formed, and emits a deprecation warning on create if the runtime is labeled as deprecated.

webhooks.name: validator.trainjob.trainer.kubeflow.org
webhooks.purpose: Validates TrainJob resources on create and update by resolving the referenced runtime (e.g., ClusterTrainingRuntime) from an in-memory registry and delegating to its ValidateObjects method, rejecting the request if the runtime is unsupported or validation fails.

webhooks.name: validator.trainingruntime.trainer.kubeflow.org
webhooks.purpose: Validates TrainingRuntime resources on create to ensure that replicated jobs with ancestor labels (trainer, model-initializer, dataset-initializer) always have exactly 1 replica and contain the required container name corresponding to their ancestor type (e.g., 'node' for trainer, 'model-initializer' for model-initializer).

webhooks.name: localmodelnamespacecache.kserve-webhook-server.validator
webhooks.purpose: Validates LocalModelNamespaceCache resources on create, update, and delete. On create/update, it ensures all referenced node groups exist as LocalModelNodeGroup resources. On delete, it prevents removal if any InferenceService in the namespace still references the cache via label matching.

webhooks.name: conversion.dscinitializations.dscinitialization.opendatahub.io
webhooks.purpose: Implements CRD conversion webhook logic to convert DSCInitialization custom resources between API version v1 (spoke) and v2 (hub), mapping spec fields (ApplicationsNamespace, Monitoring, TrustedCABundle, DevFlags) and status fields bidirectionally so that clients using either API version see a consistent resource.
